componentDescription: Select is the lower-level primitive dropdown component. Use SingleSelect or MultiSelect for form fields — they provide labels, error states, and accessibility. Use Select directly only when you need a minimal dropdown without the form-field wrapper, such as a toolbar control or custom-composed UI.
compositionPattern: Provide a trigger element (the rendered button/control) and items array. Select handles the dropdown portal and item selection. For form contexts, prefer SingleSelect or MultiSelect which layer label/error/hint text on top.

usageExamples.title: Toolbar dropdown selector
usageExamples.description: Minimal select for a toolbar without form-field chrome.
usageExamples.code:
<Select
  trigger={
    <Button
      buttonType={ButtonType.SECONDARY}
      size={ButtonSize.SMALL}
      text={selectedView}
      trailingIcon={<ChevronDownIcon />}
    />
  }
  selected={selectedView}
  onSelect={setSelectedView}
  items={[
    { items: [
      { label: 'List view', value: 'list' },
      { label: 'Grid view', value: 'grid' },
      { label: 'Table view', value: 'table' },
    ]}
  ]}
/>

usageExamples.title: Multi-select without form wrapper
usageExamples.description: Multi-select dropdown for a filter bar without label/error chrome.
usageExamples.code:
<Select
  allowMultiSelect
  trigger={<Button buttonType={ButtonType.SECONDARY} size={ButtonSize.SMALL} text={`Status (${selected.length})`} />}
  selected={selected}
  onSelect={setSelected}
  items={[{ items: statusOptions }]}
/>

trigger.llmContext: React node rendered as the trigger element. Unlike SingleSelect where the trigger is built-in, Select gives you full control over the trigger appearance. Use any Button variant, a text element, or a custom component.
trigger.propDescription: React node rendered as the dropdown trigger

items.llmContext: Required. Same SelectMenuGroupType[] structure as SingleSelect/MultiSelect. Each group: { groupLabel?, items: SelectMenuItemType[], showSeparator? }. Each item: { label, value, disabled?, slot1?, slot2? }.
items.propDescription: Required. Grouped array of dropdown items

allowMultiSelect.llmContext: Set to true to enable multi-select mode where multiple items can be checked. When using multi-select, pass selected as a string[] and onSelect receives a string[].

asModal.llmContext: Set to true to render the dropdown as a modal overlay instead of a positioned popup. Use in scrollable containers where the dropdown might clip, or on mobile where a full-screen modal provides better UX.
asModal.propDescription: Renders dropdown as a modal overlay

enableSearch.llmContext: Set to true for lists with 7+ items to allow keyboard or type-to-filter search within the dropdown.
enableSearch.propDescription: Adds a search input within the dropdown
